questions.question: Explain the two-way ANOVA technique. Describe the partitioning of total sum of squares, degrees of freedom, and the ANOVA table for a two-way classification with replication.
keyPoints: Definition of two-way ANOVA and when it is used
Two factors: Factor A (rows) and Factor B (columns)
Three hypotheses: main effect A, main effect B, interaction A x B
SS partitioning: SST = SSA + SSB + SSAB + SSE
Degrees of freedom for each source
F-statistics for each effect using MSE as denominator
Complete ANOVA table

questions.question: Differentiate between one-way and two-way ANOVA. When should two-way ANOVA be preferred over one-way ANOVA?
keyPoints: One-way: single factor; two-way: two factors
Two-way can test interaction effects
Two-way is preferred when two factors may jointly influence the outcome
Two-way reduces error variance by accounting for a second factor
Examples of scenarios requiring two-way ANOVA

questions.question: The following data shows productivity scores for 2 training methods across 2 shifts with 3 replicates per cell. Method 1, Shift 1: 45, 50, 48. Method 1, Shift 2: 40, 42, 38. Method 2, Shift 1: 55, 58, 52. Method 2, Shift 2: 48, 50, 46. Perform a two-way ANOVA at alpha = 0.05.
keyPoints: Compute cell totals, row totals, column totals, and grand total
Calculate CF, SST, SSA, SSB, SSAB, and SSE
Determine all degrees of freedom
Compute MS and F for each effect
Compare with F-critical values and draw conclusions for all three tests

questions.question: What is an interaction effect in two-way ANOVA? How is it tested? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Definition: effect of one factor depends on the level of the other
Interaction sum of squares SSAB
F_AB = MSAB / MSE
Graphical interpretation: non-parallel lines in interaction plot
Impact on interpreting main effects when interaction is significant
Numerical or conceptual example

questions.question: Explain two-way ANOVA without replication. Why can interaction not be tested in this case? Construct the ANOVA table for a two-way layout without replication.
keyPoints: One observation per cell (n = 1)
SST = SSA + SSB + SSE (no SSAB term)
Error df = (a-1)(b-1) absorbs interaction
Cannot estimate pure error separately from interaction
ANOVA table with three sources only
Suitable example
